title: Build Caffeine Capability Test App with Real Motoko Backend Features

requirements.text: Create a dark-themed single-page app layout with a grid of 11 feature test cards, each displaying a status badge (PASS / LOCAL-ONLY / NOT SUPPORTED) and clear explanations for any limitations

quotes: Clean dark UI
Each feature in a separate card
Each card must show a status badge: PASS / LOCAL-ONLY / NOT SUPPORTED

acceptanceCriteria: Dark color scheme applied consistently across the app
All 11 feature cards are visible in a responsive grid layout
Each card displays a colored status badge
Limitation explanations are clearly visible where applicable

requirements.text: Implement Local Persistence Test card with a todo input field, save to localStorage, persist after browser refresh, and include a clear storage button

quotes: Small todo input
Save to localStorage
Persist after refresh
Clear storage button

acceptanceCriteria: Todo input field accepts and stores text in localStorage
Stored todos persist after page refresh
Clear button removes all todos from localStorage
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Create Motoko backend method to store and retrieve a global counter value that persists across all users and devices

quotes: i want to build a potential leader board and cross-device sync
everything should be real

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores a single global counter in stable memory
Increment method increases counter and returns new value
Get method returns current counter value
Counter persists across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Implement Cross-Device Sync Test card with an Increment button that calls the Motoko backend to update a global counter visible to all users across all devices

quotes: Global counter with Increment button
Attempt to persist globally across devices
i want to build a potential leader board and cross-device sync

acceptanceCriteria: Button calls backend increment method
Counter value updates in real-time after increment
Multiple users see the same counter value
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Create Motoko backend methods to add leaderboard entries (name and score) and retrieve all entries sorted by score in descending order, with global persistence across all users

quotes: Attempt global leaderboard shared across users
i want to build a potential leader board and cross-device sync
everything should be real

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores leaderboard entries in stable memory
Add method accepts name and score
Get method returns entries sorted by score descending
Leaderboard persists across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Implement Leaderboard Test card with a form to submit name and score, display the global leaderboard from the Motoko backend sorted by score, and refresh the list after each submission

quotes: Name + score form
Attempt global leaderboard shared across users

acceptanceCriteria: Form accepts name and numeric score input
Submit button calls backend add method
Leaderboard displays all entries sorted by score
Multiple users see the same leaderboard data
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Implement External API Fetch Test card that fetches data from a public REST API (e.g., JSONPlaceholder or similar) and displays the JSON result, with error handling for blocked requests

quotes: Fetch data from a public public API
Display JSON result
If external requests are blocked, show error and label NOT SUPPORTED

acceptanceCriteria: Button triggers fetch to a public API endpoint
Successful response displays formatted JSON
Network errors display clear error message
Card shows PASS if successful, NOT SUPPORTED if blocked

requirements.text: Create Motoko backend method that returns a test JSON response to demonstrate backend API endpoint functionality

quotes: Attempt to create a simple API endpoint (e.g., /api/test returning JSON {status: "ok"})

acceptanceCriteria: Backend method returns a structured response object
Response includes status field and timestamp
Method is callable from frontend

requirements.text: Implement API Route/Backend Test card that calls the Motoko backend test method and displays the JSON response, with explanation that Motoko canisters provide backend functionality instead of traditional REST routes

quotes: Attempt to create a simple API endpoint
If backend routes are not supported, clearly explain limitation

acceptanceCriteria: Button calls backend test method
Response displays formatted JSON
Explanation describes Motoko canister method architecture
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Implement Email Capability Test card with a form (name and message fields), generate a mailto link pre-populated with To: brianvbn@gmail.com, Subject: 'Caffeine Test Submission - [Name]', and Body including name, message, timestamp, and random session ID, display the generated link, and include a 'Send via Email Client' button with explanation that direct email requires external services

quotes: Form with name + message
To: brianvbn@gmail.com
Subject: "Caffeine Test Submission - [Name]"
Body: Include: Name, Message, Timestamp, Random session ID
Show the generated mailto link before clicking
Button: "Send via Email Client"
If direct email sending requires paid integrations, explain and implement mailto fallback

acceptanceCriteria: Form captures name and message input
Generated mailto link is displayed as text
Link includes all specified fields with proper encoding
Button opens default email client with pre-filled content
Explanation clarifies mailto is a fallback approach
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Integrate Internet Identity authentication with login/logout functionality, protect specific feature cards requiring authentication, and display the authenticated user's principal ID

quotes: Simple login gate
Attempt server-side auth
make real auth as well pls, no simulations anywhere

acceptanceCriteria: Login button triggers Internet Identity authentication flow
After login, user's principal ID is displayed
Protected cards are only accessible when authenticated
Logout button clears authentication state
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Create Motoko backend methods to upload image files as byte arrays with metadata (filename, content type, uploader principal), store them globally, and retrieve all uploaded images with their data

quotes: Upload image and preview
Attempt to persist so other users can see it
fil upload, everything should be real

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores uploaded images in stable memory
Upload method accepts image bytes, filename, and content type
Method associates uploaded image with authenticated principal
Get method returns all uploaded images globally
Images persist across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Implement File Upload Test card with image upload input, preview uploaded image locally, call Motoko backend to persist the image globally, and display all globally uploaded images from all users in a gallery view

quotes: Upload image and preview
Attempt to persist so other users can see it
fil upload, everything should be real

acceptanceCriteria: File input accepts image uploads
Selected image displays local preview
Upload button sends image to backend
Gallery displays all globally uploaded images
Multiple users see the same uploaded images
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Implement Real-Time Test card that explains WebSockets and Socket.io are not supported on the Internet Computer platform, display NOT SUPPORTED status badge, and clarify that polling or query-based updates are alternative patterns

quotes: Attempt simple two-user live counter or chat
If real-time is not supported, clearly label NOT SUPPORTED

acceptanceCriteria: Card displays NOT SUPPORTED status badge
Explanation clearly states WebSocket limitations
Alternative polling pattern is mentioned
No simulated real-time functionality is implemented

requirements.text: Implement Version/Deploy Test card displaying app version number 'v1.0', include an editable changelog textarea that persists locally, and show the current deployment environment information

quotes: Display app version number (v1.0)
Editable changelog section

acceptanceCriteria: Version number 'v1.0' is prominently displayed
Changelog textarea allows editing
Changelog content persists in localStorage
Deployment environment info is shown
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Implement URL State/Query Parameter Test card that reads query parameters from the browser URL (specifically checking for ?code=237), unlocks a hidden section when the correct value is detected, displays all detected query parameters on screen, and explains that URL state works reliably in React

quotes: Read a query parameter from the URL (example: ?code=237)
If correct value is present, unlock hidden section
Display detected query parameters on screen
Explain whether URL-based state works reliably

acceptanceCriteria: Query parameters are parsed from URL on component mount
All query parameters are displayed in a list
Hidden section becomes visible when ?code=237 is present
Explanation confirms URL state works in React Router
Card displays PASS status badge

requirements.text: Apply a creative visual theme with distinct colors (avoiding blue and purple), typography, and layout style that creates a cohesive, modern dark UI experience across all feature cards

quotes: Clean dark UI

acceptanceCriteria: Consistent color palette applied across all cards
Typography hierarchy is clear and readable
Card layouts follow a unified design system
Dark theme is implemented without relying on blue/purple

constraints: All backend logic must be implemented in a single Motoko actor
No simulated functionality - all features must be real implementations or clearly labeled as NOT SUPPORTED
Authentication must use Internet Identity (passkeys, Google, Apple, Microsoft)
File uploads must persist globally using Motoko backend storage
Real-time features (WebSockets/Socket.io) are not supported and must be labeled NOT SUPPORTED
nonGoals: Implementing simulated or fake global functionality
Adding REST-style backend routes (use Motoko canister methods instead)
Direct email sending via SMTP or third-party services
Using blue and purple color schemes unless explicitly requested
